name: Castro Las Espinillas y La virgen de las Espinillas – desde Valdeavellano de Tera
region_intro: Hiking around Valdeavellano De Tera offers access to varied landscapes within the Soria province. The region features a mix of oak forests, rolling hills, and open terrain, providing diverse hiking experiences. Trails often lead to historical sites like ancient castros or hermitages, and some routes explore natural features such as lagoons. The area is characterized by its rural setting and natural beauty, making it suitable for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Valdeavellano De Tera The most popular hiking route is Templo del Roble – circular desde El Royo, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a steady ascent through woodland. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro Las Espinillas y La virgen de las Espinillas – desde Valdeavellano de Tera, a moderate 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail explores a historical fort site and offers views of the surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Cebollera Lagoon loop from Laguna, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through natural parkland, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Hiking around Valdeavellano De Tera is defined by oak forests, historical sites, and the serene landscapes of the Soria region. The network offers options for easy family friendly walks as well as more challenging routes for experienced hikers. The routes in Valdeavellano De Tera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdeavellano De Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes documented around Valdeavellano De Tera, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging treks.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: Yes, Valdeavellano De Tera offers several easy and family-friendly walks. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon loop from Laguna&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.7 miles (4.4 km) long and suitable for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Valdeavellano De Tera area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063896339/templo-del-roble-circular-desde-el-royo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templo del Roble – circular desde El Royo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) loop that takes you through woodland.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on hikes around Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: The region features diverse natural beauty. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2089062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, discover various &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5608993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the unique ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acebal de Garagüeta Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can hike to the ancient fort site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063880639/castro-las-espinillas-y-la-virgen-de-las-espinillas-desde-valdeavellano-de-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Las Espinillas&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3942148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosa de la Sierra Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon loop from Laguna&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes, while more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062391615/ermita-santos-nuevos-y-alto-de-tabanera-circular-desde-san-andres-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 6 hours.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: The trails around Valdeavellano De Tera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, from oak forests to rolling hills, and the serene, rural setting.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking trails in Valdeavellano De Tera?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6911218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Terrazas Heights&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint where you can enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding Soria province.

Q: What are some of the more challenging hikes available for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062391615/ermita-santos-nuevos-y-alto-de-tabanera-circular-desde-san-andres-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14-mile (22.5 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks or reserves near Valdeavellano De Tera that are good for hiking?
A: Yes, the area is close to natural parkland, particularly around the Cebollera Lagoon. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24312181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon loop from Bercolar&lt;/a&gt; take you directly through these protected natural areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Valdeavellano De Tera is varied, featuring a mix of oak forests, rolling hills, and open landscapes. Trails often involve steady ascents and descents, with paths ranging from well-trodden tracks to more rugged sections, typical of a rural mountain environment.
tips: Beautiful Soriano town known as Valdeavellano de Tera, where we can see the Catholic parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Paz, patron saint of Valdeavellano de Tera. One km from the town we can visit the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de las Espinillas.
Located on a hillside, the Castro de Las Espinillas was in the past, a fortified town (S.VI and V ac). In the place we will find an information panel that will give us more details about the place.
